old_claim.note: X-Ray Imaging provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: CT reconstructs cross-sectional body images from many X-ray attenuation measurements.

source_shape.source_locator: Nobel 1979 press-release description of computerized transverse axial tomography using X-ray measurements.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes computed tomography as reconstructing internal cross sections from many X-ray measurements made around the body.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a required edge because X-ray attenuation measurements are the physical input for the scoped CT imaging modality.

ontology_before: The graph treated X-ray imaging as a soft enabler for CT, which understated CT's dependence on X-ray measurements.
ontology_after: CT now has X-ray imaging as the hard modality foundation, while computers and DSP remain reconstruction enablers.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: CT's X-ray edge is required rather than a generic enabling edge.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to include non-X-ray tomography such as MRI or ultrasound tomography.
A source showed ordinary computed tomography does not require X-ray attenuation measurements.
The graph removed computing and reconstruction context while strengthening the X-ray edge.
short_reason: Medical CT is an X-ray tomography modality, so X-ray imaging is a hard method dependency.
